column1: On behalf of our entire Disco staff, I'd like to express our appreciation for your patronage this evening. We hope you enjoyed your visit, Sir!
column2: 「代表本舞廳全體員工，感謝您今晚賞光。祝您玩得盡興，先生！」

column1: (Boy, has his tune changed, eh Larry?)
column2: （咦，這傢伙態度轉變也太快了吧，拉瑞？）

column1: This disco looks like a very swinging place, you say to Pickhinke, "how's about letting me in?"
column2: 「這間舞廳看起來嗨翻天，」你對 Pickhinke 說，「讓我進去嘛，拜託？」

column1: Either show your membership card, or get lost, Scum-Breath!
column2: 「要嘛亮出會員卡，要嘛滾一邊去，臭酸鬼！」

column1: (It seems he's uninterested in idle chit-chat, Larry.)
column2: （看來他沒興趣跟你打嘴炮，拉瑞。）

column1: Excuse me, sir, you say to the bouncer, "but I really have to use the restroom. Would it be okay for me to use the one inside?"
column2: 「嗯，老兄，」你對保鏢說，「我真的急著上廁所。能讓我用裡面的嗎？」

column1: Nope, he replies, succinctly.
column2: 「不行，」他惜字如金地回答。

column1: The bouncer smells of stale gymnasiums and vinyl exercise benches.
column2: 保鏢身上一股陳年健身房配人造皮椅的餿味。

column1: How's about a little green sliding across your palm? you ask the bouncer.
column2: 「要不要讓幾個『小朋友』在你手心滑一下？」你問保鏢。

column1: How's about your little butt sliding across the pavement? he replies.
column2: 「你那顆小屁股在柏油路上滑一滑比較快啦。」他回嘴。

column1: Hey, get that crap away from me!
column2: 「喂，那什麼鬼東西，拿開啦！」

column1: Wanna know what time it is? you ask the bouncer.
column2: 「想知道現在幾點嗎？」你問保鏢。

column1: Time for you to get lost, Creep!

column1: Wanna nibble on my fruit? you ask the bouncer.
column2: 「想咬一口我的水果嗎？」你問保鏢。

column1: Wanna taste of my knuckles? he responds.

column1: I never drink when I'm on duty, Pickhinke replies.
column2: 「上班時間，滴酒不沾，」Pickhinke 說。

column1: Have you ever made a balloon out one of these?
column2: 「你有用這個吹過氣球嗎？」

column1: Pickhinke ignores you completely.
column2: Pickhinke 完全無視你。

column1: Wanna nibble on my num nums?
column2: 「想咬咬我的美食嗎？」

column1: You gotta be kiddin!
column2: 「你是在唬爛吧！」

column1: Your attempt to stab the bouncer is foolish. He handles tough guys for a living.
column2: 你想戳保鏢是蠢事。他天天跟硬漢打交道。

column1: I never drink when I'm on duty, he replies.
column2: 「上班時間，滴酒不沾，」他說。

column1: Take a look at these!
column2: 「瞧瞧這些！」

column1: Man, I got better than that waiting at home, he replies.

column1: May I offer you one of these breath mints? you ask the bouncer.

column1: Right. Sure. Do I look stupid?
column2: 「是喔，好啊。我臉上是不是寫著『盤仔』？」

column1: (Don't answer that, Larry! It may be a trick question.)
column2: （別回答，拉瑞！這絕對是陷阱題。）

column1: Who's Ken? barks the bouncer.
column2: 「誰是 Ken？」保鏢吠道。

column1: Try as you might, you are unable to "turn on" the fireplug.
column2: 你再怎麼努力也轉不開這個消防栓。

column2: 鎖著的大門有效地阻止了你去追求你所渴望的文化啟蒙。

column1: You smile at the girl and say, "May I join you?"
column2: 你對女孩微笑說，「我可以加入你嗎？」

column1: And, of course she replies, "Why, am I coming apart?"

column1: Perhaps you should sit with her first.
column2: 也許你應該先坐下來。

column1: Not again. That trip through the ceiling gave me a headache!
column2: 「又來！那次穿過天花板的經驗讓我頭疼！」

column1: You screw up your courage and ask, "What would you say to a spin around the dance floor with the nearest thing to John Travolta, baby?"
column2: 你鼓起勇氣問，「寶貝，要不要跟全場最像 John Travolta 的男人在舞池裡轉一圈？」

column1: Well, okay, I guess, she replies, thinking to herself, "near as I'm gonna get!"
column2: 「好啦，我想可以，」她說著，心裡想著「大概也就這個貨色了。」

column1: Hey, Baby, what's a beautiful doll like you doing sitting all alone? you say to the beautiful doll sitting all alone. "Wanna dance?"
column2: 「嗨，寶貝，妳這麼漂亮怎麼一個人坐著？」你對著獨自坐著的美女說，「想跳舞嗎？」

column1: Dance? With you? she replies, "Why I don't even know you."
column2: 「跳舞？跟你？」她回道，「我根本不認識你。」

column1: Once again, you're a little premature, Larry!

column1: The girl smells of expensive perfume and has even more expensive tastes.
column2: 女孩身上飄著昂貴香水味，品味更是貴得嚇人。

column1: Gosh, Larry. Maybe you should have loaned her some money!
column2: 天哪，拉瑞。也許你該借她點錢！

column1: There's a gorgeous blonde sitting at the table, and she's all alone!

column1: (The preceding was a paid editorial message.)

column1: But, of course, Larry, Fawn replies, "You should have asked sooner!"

column2: 她繼續說，「不過首先，我們必須結婚。我絕不會跟一個不是我丈夫的男人親熱。如果你能借我兩百塊，我就租賭場飯店的蜜月套房。然後在那裡舉辦婚禮後慶祝。」

column2: 哇塞，正到炸！她有雙電眼藍瞳和迷死人的笑容。你的視線忍不住上下打量，看到的每一吋都合你胃口。她簡直銷魂到爆！！

column1: (Larry, this MUST be love!)
column2: （Larry，這一定是愛！）

column2: 她微笑著說，「我就是個沒法說不的女孩…

column1: Oh, %s is sweet, but I'd like something special... from a guy like you!
column2: 「哦，%s 很可愛，不過我想要點特別的……來自你這樣的傢伙！」

column1: Her mind is stuck in the present(s).

column1: A long and lovely neck perfectly fills the distance between her head and her shoulders!
column2: 一條長長的、迷人的脖子完美地填滿了她頭部和肩膀之間的距離！

column1: Glancing at your watch, you realize the terrible truth. Your night in Lost Wages is over, and (technically speaking) you're still...
column2: 瞥了一眼手錶，你意識到一個可怕的事實。你在失樂城的夜晚結束了，而且（嚴格來說）你還是……

column1: Larry-cakes anyone?

column2: （這還是它的優點呢！）

column1: Vowing to hold your breath forever, you enter this miserable excuse for public transportation.

column1: Obviously, he thinks you're a fire plug.

column1: The dog smells quite bad, although still better than you.
column2: 那隻狗身上臭到不行，不過說來慚愧，還是比你香。
